description:
This table displays a list of available vector layers for selection in a user interface titled "Select Vector Layers to Add...". The table has five columns: "Layer ID", "Layer name", "Number of features", "Geometry type", and "Description".

| Layer ID | Layer name | Number of features | Geometry type | Description |
| :------- | :------------------ | :----------------- | :------------ | :---------- |
| 2        | LISTADO_ATRIBUTOS   | 20                 | None          |             |
| 3        | LISTADO_COBERTURAS  | 62                 | None          |             |
| 1        | LISTADO_USOS        | 87                 | None          |             |
| 0        | SAR_13_TABLA_PLANA  | 9445               | None          |             |
| 5        | SAR_13_T_COMBINADA  | 981049             | MultiPolygon  |             |
| 6        | SAR_13_T_POLIGONOS  | 1963199            | MultiPolygon  |             |
| 7        | SAR_13_T_USOS       | 844040             | MultiPolygon  |             |
| 4        | SAR_13_T_VALORES    | 2455265            | None          |             |

The "Geometry type" column indicates whether a layer consists of MultiPolygon features or "None" (implying tabular or non-spatial data). Layers with 'SAR_13' in their name, such as "SAR_13_T_POLIGONOS", "SAR_13_T_COMBINADA", and "SAR_13_T_USOS", are MultiPolygon geometry types and contain a large number of features, with "SAR_13_T_POLIGONOS" having the most at 1,963,199 features. Layers like "LISTADO_ATRIBUTOS" (List of Attributes), "LISTADO_COBERTURAS" (List of Coverages), and "LISTADO_USOS" (List of Uses) have fewer features and a geometry type of "None".
